$comment: Machine-readable project status. Updated as part of significant implementation work. NOT a substitute for running the tests or reading the source - see HANDOFF.md and AGENTS.md.

phase.name: Functional first version
phase.next_name: Document extraction, indexing, investigation mode, memory

quality_gates.mypy: clean (52 source files, strict on security-sensitive modules)

partial.missing: Model-assisted summarisation. Deliberately deferred: a mechanical drop is predictable, a generated summary can invent things the user did not say.
Freedesktop trash spec for Linux. Falls back to an explicit permanent-delete confirmation elsewhere.
Nothing stores a secret yet. Becomes real with the local API auth token.

planned.notes: Extractor Protocol designed in docs/architecture.md. Optional deps already declared under [extract]; doctor already reports which are missing.
Embeddings optional, local models preferred, indexes stored locally.
memories table exists and is intentionally empty. Must be opt-in, inspectable, deletable. Never hidden.
MUST reuse PermissionEngine. No second permission path.
stdio first. Interface.MCP and Caller.client_id already flow through the engine and audit log.
Optional module. Detect hardware, estimate memory, require confirmation, log config, support resume.

fixed_during_phase_1.summary: push_screen_wait requires a Textual worker context. It was called from ordinary async methods, so /models crashed with NoActiveWorker and -- far worse -- the permission confirmation dialog crashed, meaning every tool call in Manual mode failed with a traceback instead of asking the user.
fixed_during_phase_1.fix: Decorated _run_turn, action_select_model, action_select_mode, action_select_theme and _confirm_bypass with @work.
fixed_during_phase_1.why_it_was_missed: Runner tests stubbed the confirm callback; TUI tests never triggered one.

fixed_during_phase_1.summary: PromptArea defined a nested Changed message, shadowing TextArea.Changed. TextArea posts its own as self.Changed(self), so it was constructed through our __init__ and arrived carrying the widget instead of the text.
App.query_one searches the *active* screen. Refreshing the top bar while a modal was open raised NoMatches and killed the turn worker mid-confirmation.
fixed_during_phase_1.fix: Bar widgets cached at mount and accessed through properties.

fixed_during_phase_1.summary: ThinkingIndicator.stop() returned the reactive  attribute, which only advances when the interval timer fires, so a turn finishing between ticks reported zero seconds.
fixed_during_phase_1.fix: stop() computes the duration from the start time directly.

fixed_during_phase_1.summary: RiskLevel subclasses StrEnum and only defined __lt__/__le__, so >= fell back to alphabetical string comparison. 'read' >= 'execute' was True, making the kill switch block ordinary reads and workspace mode demand allow_execute for a plain write.
fixed_during_phase_1.fix: All four comparison operators defined explicitly against rank.

fixed_during_phase_1.summary: DirEntry.stat() on Windows returns cached FindFirstFile data with st_ino and st_dev both 0. The (dev, ino) cycle-detection key matched every directory, so recursive walks silently descended only one level.
fixed_during_phase_1.fix: Cycle detection keyed on resolved paths, and only applied when following links.

fixed_during_phase_1.summary: sqlite3.executescript commits any open transaction before running, so the migration runner's outer BEGIN/COMMIT had nothing to commit and every migration failed.
fixed_during_phase_1.fix: Transaction control moved inside the script, which also handles semicolons in CREATE TRIGGER bodies.

fixed_during_phase_1.summary: Textual's TextArea handles keys in its own _on_key and consumes Enter, so the App-level handler was dead code and Enter inserted a newline instead of sending.
fixed_during_phase_1.fix: PromptArea subclass intercepts in _on_key and posts a Submitted message.

fixed_during_phase_1.summary: The role-impersonation pattern did not allow the pipe delimiter, so <|system|> and <|im_start|> - the most common template-injection form for local models - were not detected.
The 'did you mean read_file?' suggestion lived in ToolNotFoundError.remediation, which the runner discarded when building the failure message, so the model never saw it and could not self-correct.
fixed_during_phase_1.fix: Runner folds remediation into the message returned to the model.

fixed_during_phase_1.summary: A method named list() shadowed the builtin inside its own class body, breaking every '-> list[...]' annotation after it.

concerns.notes: Detection is heuristic and documented as such. The guarantee is the permissions engine: a successful injection still cannot make a consequential change without confirmation. Content fencing is unconditional and cannot be disabled.
Requires a typed confirmation phrase and is unreachable by keystroke. Still logs, displays and audits everything, and protected paths remain unwritable.
Conversations and audit logs are plain SQLite/JSONL protected only by filesystem permissions. Documented in docs/security-model.md.
Enabling it permits junction traversal out of a workspace. doctor flags it as a warning.

verified_environment.end_to_end_verified: Real tool-calling turn against the auto-selected model (qwen3.6:27b): tool requested, permission evaluated, executed, result returned, model answered. Exact token counts reported by Ollama. Also verified on qwen3:8b at ~43 tok/s.
